full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Near the height of this nation's history of lynching and other forms of racist violence,<|7.86|> <|7.86|> a multiracial revival broke out on Azusa Street in Los Angeles in 1906.<|15.62|> <|16.44|> It's a long time ago.<|17.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> ago. The Azusa Street Revival is understood as the birth event of 20th century Pentecostalism,<|9.98|> <|9.98|> the fastest growing form of Christianity in Africa, South America, Asia, and the Pacific.<|18.30|> <|19.80|> The outpouring of the Holy Spirit from 1906 into the 1920s was an invigorating movement of<|28.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> God, a snapshot of what is possible when Christians are open to God's relentless,<|7.90|> <|8.84|> irresistible pursuit of us in Christ. But what began as a revival of Christianity<|16.16|> <|16.16|> fractured and splintered, and we all know this story, into two large denominations<|23.26|> <|23.26|> based on lines of race, not church.<|27.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This division and these two denominations came directly out of the Azusa Street Revival.<|8.00|> <|8.00|> This division is especially
